---
slug: marketing
display_name: Marketing Voice
version: "1.0"

prohibited_terms:
  - "cheap"
  - "guaranteed returns"
  - "risk-free"
  - /(?i)unlimited\s+free/
  - /(?i)act\s+now/

target_avg_sentence_length_words: 18
flesch_kincaid_reading_level_max: 10

we_ratio_max: 0.3

tone_required_signals:
  - confident
  - helpful
  - clear

tone_forbidden_signals:
  - fear
  - urgency_artificial
---

## Voice principles

Speak to the reader as a knowledgeable peer. We are here to help them succeed,
not to impress them with jargon or push them into decisions.

- Use active voice. "You can do X" beats "X can be done."
- One idea per sentence. Short paragraphs.
- Numbers and specifics beat adjectives. "Saves 3 hours a week" beats "saves time."
- Contractions are fine: "you'll", "we're", "it's".

## Prohibited constructions

- Superlatives without evidence: "the best", "the fastest" — only use with data.
- Passive voice in CTAs: "Click here to get started" not "Get started by clicking here."
- Fear-based language: "don't miss out", "before it's too late".

## Examples

Good: "Export your data in three steps — no engineering needed."
Avoid: "Data can be exported by users via the export function at any time."

Good: "You'll save roughly 3 hours a week on manual reporting."
Avoid: "Massive time savings are guaranteed for all users."
